query: Nintendo Switch 2 discontinued original Switch family Europe date February 2027 official source

{"query":"Nintendo Switch 2 discontinued original Switch family Europe date February 2027 official source","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1763,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://famiboards.com/threads/nintendo-announces-the-end-of-new-stock-for-the-nintendo-switch-family-of-systems-original-lite-and-oled-in-europe-starting-mid-february-2027.18004/","title":"Nintendo announces the end of new stock for the Nintendo Switch Family of Systems (Original, Lite, and OLED) in Europe starting Mid-February 2027 | Famiboards","content":"From mid-February 2027, almost ten years after Nintendo Switch launched in March 2017, Nintendo will no longer sell to retailers hardware in the Nintendo Switch family of systems – specifically Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch Lite and Nintendo Switch – OLED Model.
The 10-year plan is complete (in Europe)
The Switch 1 is still amongst the most successful consoles in all history; them not overtaking a revised sales number reported 15-years after the PS2 was discontinued doesn't impact that in the slightest.
This kinda makes me think Rhythm Heaven Groove truly is the last first party title for the original Switch.
I think Japan will be the last region where NSW is sold.
tbh i expect it to end in NOA and other markets the year after tbh.
Catalog games sell, but they're generally not pushing the numbers of Tomodachi Life or Rhythm Heaven anymore.
Going farther out than that... looks like the last SNES model was slightly more than a year beyond N64.
The craziest, though, the late Famicom/NES design coming about 3 years after SNES."},{"url":"https://www.nintendo.com/en-gb/Support/Nintendo-Switch-2/Information-about-upcoming-battery-related-revisions-to-some-Nintendo-products-3132901.html","title":"Information about upcoming battery-related revisions to some Nintendo products | Support | Nintendo UK","content":"Starting summer 2026, in preparation for upcoming changes in European battery regulations coming into effect in mid-February 2027, selected Nintendo products in Europe will begin to be replaced on a rolling basis by revisions that contain a user-replaceable battery.
The first revised products are expected to become available from summer 2026, with additional products becoming available in autumn, winter, and early 2027.
More information will be shared shortly before each revised product becomes available.
Nintendo will no longer offer the above-named products on Nintendo Store after mid-February 2027.
Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch Lite, and Nintendo Switch – OLED Model will all continue to be manufactured in 2026, and should be widely available in Europe all year."},{"url":"https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/07/nintendo-will-stop-selling-the-original-switch-from-february-2027","title":"Nintendo Will Stop Selling The Original Switch From February 2027 | Nintendo Life","content":"Update [Tue 7th Jul 2026, 12:45pm]: Nintendo has provided a statement to IGN clarifying that it won't be withdrawing the Switch, Switch Lite, or Switch - OLED Model from sale outside of Europe.
"We plan to continue selling Nintendo Switch in regions outside of Nintendo of Europe."
"Regarding availability at retail, please check with your local retailers in the future for more information."},{"url":"https://mynintendonews.com/2026/07/06/original-nintendo-switch-family-of-systems-will-be-discontinued-mid-february-2027/","title":"Original Nintendo Switch family of systems will be discontinued mid-February 2027 - My Nintendo News","content":"Update: Nintendo has told IGN that it will still continue to sell the Nintendo Switch family of systems in certain markets outside of Europe after this date.
This will mean that the original Nintendo Switch family of systems will have been made available for ten years."}]}

Nintendo’s approach now will balance compliance with Europe’s new laws and preserving the existing Nintendo Switch 2 experience, both in the console and its accessories. The first product players can expect to see these changes happen with select Joy-Con colors in the summer, and they will have user-replaceable batteries without changing the battery capacity or weight specifications. Following this, the major change will be with the Nintendo Switch 2 console in the fall of 2026.
